tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject IDs, sex, treatment, toxicity, hematologic toxicity, neutropenia, anemia, thrombocytopenia, neuropathy, progression-free survival, Kaplan Meier-Censor, race, ethnicity, histology, type of disease, stage of cancer, smoking history of patient, age of patient during trial, number of prior therapies, and genotypes.

variables.name: AVERAGE_NUMBER_CHEW_POUCHES
variables.description: Average number of tobbaco packages chewed

variables.name: AVERAGE_NUMBER_SECOND_HAND_EXPOSURE
variables.description: Average number of times patient exposed to second hand smoke

variables.name: NUMBER_YEARS_EXPOSED_SECOND_HAND_TOBACCO
variables.description: Average number of years patient exposed to second hand smoke

variables.name: SECOND_HAND_TOBACCO
variables.description: Has the patient been exposed to second hand smoke

variables.name: SMOKED_PIPE
variables.description: Has the patient smoked a pipe

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample IDs, body site where the sample was taken, analyte type, histological type, and tumor status.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: The body site from which the sample was taken

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject IDs, consent information, and case control status of the subject for lung cancer. All subjects are cancer patients.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of the subject for lung cancer; All subjects are cancer patients.

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
